title: Multidisciplinary Review Packet Template v0

target_artifacts.review_goal: Confirm that the staged pipeline keeps case-specific work private and public artifacts generic.
Confirm that public field groups cannot be mistaken for a case upload schema.
Confirm that context modifiers remain optional, source-scoped, non-identifying, and cannot produce prognosis, eligibility, treatment, or option ranking.
Confirm that review-readiness scoring cannot become candidate ranking or action guidance.
Confirm that publication gates fail closed before case-derived public learning is released.
Confirm that MRD, relapse, target, and response terms are precise enough for review packet routing.
Confirm that mechanism buckets stay navigation-oriented and do not become mechanism rankings.
Confirm that trial-query provenance stays separate from eligibility, availability, and recommendation claims.

review_status_boundary: The public template and linked artifacts are source-checked review infrastructure.
This template does not make any artifact expert-reviewed.
Expert-reviewed status requires completed comments from qualified reviewers and a follow-up artifact update.
Blocked status means material must remain private or be rewritten when privacy, provenance, scope, review status, or clinical-use boundary is incomplete.

review_questions: Are disease-state, response, relapse, and MRD terms sufficiently bounded for review packet routing?
Does any language imply prognosis, treatment selection, or patient-specific interpretation?
Which source IDs and measurement terms must be present before public wording can be strengthened?
review_items.reviewer_action_needed: Confirm disease-state and measurement wording boundaries before any private case review is summarized publicly.

review_questions: Are high-risk, extramedullary, organ, frailty, performance-status, immune-status, infection-risk, and prior-exposure fields optional and source-scoped?
Could any context field imply prognosis, eligibility, treatment selection, monitoring, or option ranking?
Which context fields must remain private-only, synthetic-only, or expert-review-needed before public reuse?
review_items.reviewer_action_needed: Confirm context modifier wording boundaries before any private case context is summarized publicly.
safety_boundary: Not a treatment or trial recommendation.

review_questions: Are CAR T, bispecific, alternate-target, and immune-effector concepts separated from clinical action language?
Does the packet preserve preclinical, translational, and registry boundaries?
Does any wording imply a mechanism ranking or treatment path?
review_items.reviewer_action_needed: Review cellular-therapy wording before any review prompt is reused in a private packet.

review_questions: Are specimen source, assay method, scoring threshold, timing, and denominator context preserved?
Which measurement fields must block cross-source comparison when missing?
Does the packet avoid treating target status alone as treatment guidance?
review_items.reviewer_action_needed: Confirm minimum measurement fields before private packet findings can be summarized generically.

review_questions: Are genomic, target-density, and model-system statements labeled with source maturity?
Does the packet keep target biology separate from actionability?
Which primary sources are needed before public claim language is strengthened?
review_items.reviewer_action_needed: Review whether source maturity and target-biology limits are sufficiently visible.

review_questions: Does the packet preserve trial-query provenance without implying eligibility?
Are recruitment status, site availability, and criteria verification explicitly left to trial sites and treating clinicians?
Does any public wording imply a trial recommendation?
review_items.reviewer_action_needed: Confirm that trial-query records remain public registry-pattern artifacts only.

review_questions: Does the packet avoid implying regulatory availability or sponsor willingness?
Are expanded-access and investigational-product pathways described only as generic review domains?
Are institutional, legal, sponsor, and regulatory decisions explicitly outside the public repo?
review_items.reviewer_action_needed: Confirm that access-pathway language stays generic and non-advisory.

review_questions: Are toxicity, immune risk, logistics, privacy, and consent boundaries visible without becoming instructions?
Does the packet fail closed when safety or governance review is incomplete?
Are patient-protection concerns separated from public-source evidence strength?
review_items.reviewer_action_needed: Confirm that safety and ethics language blocks overclaiming and public release of private facts.

review_questions: Would a patient, advocate, or builder mistake any prompt for medical advice?
Are source-checked, expert-review-needed, expert-reviewed, and blocked states visibly distinct?
Does the packet avoid cure, trial, treatment, monitoring, eligibility, and access promises?
review_items.reviewer_action_needed: Review public language before any patient-facing summary or open research map depends on this packet.
safety_boundary: Not a treatment, trial, or monitoring recommendation.

completion_gate: At least one qualified reviewer must complete each selected review item before any target artifact is labeled expert-reviewed.
Reviewer comments must map back to public source IDs, claim IDs, gap IDs, measurement term IDs, query-record IDs, extraction record IDs, or task IDs.
Any claim-strength increase requires explicit reviewer support and a follow-up pull request.
Any private case-derived learning must pass the publication gate before any public artifact is prepared.
Any incomplete evidence should weaken or clarify public wording rather than strengthen it.
interpretation_boundary: This packet is review infrastructure, not completed expert review.
This packet is not medical advice, diagnostic guidance, treatment guidance, trial guidance, expanded-access guidance, monitoring guidance, or a cure claim.
This packet does not make patient-specific recommendations.
This packet must not be filled publicly with real case facts.
This packet does not upgrade source-checked artifacts to expert-reviewed artifacts.

review_notes: Reusable public template for future private multidisciplinary myeloma review packets.
Separates review readiness from completed expert review.
Uses publication gate and candidate-option rubric as upstream public-safety boundaries.
Adds a context-modifier review item for optional, source-scoped high-risk, extramedullary, organ, frailty, immune, infection-risk, and prior-exposure fields.
